what_it_does: Records ledger metadata for a design-source JPEG image asset (vt-residential/p-1) and labels it as design ground-truth.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, address, channel, path, blob URI, mime type, name, dimensions, source, author session, timestamp, and empty links
Image dimensions w and h are both 0
Body text states 'SOURCE design ground-truth — vt-residential/p-1.jpg'
standouts: w and h are 0 despite being an image/jpeg entry
Blob reference present but no descriptive caption or alt text
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter in a markdown ledger file
References assets by blob:// URI and image:// channel address
Separates machine metadata from a short human-readable source note
concerns: Zero width/height suggests image metadata was not extracted or is missing
No links or relationships captured in the ledger entry
No description, alt text, or tags for the image content
notes: A sparse ledger card for a design-source reference image, flagged as ground truth but lacking extracted dimensions and descriptive metadata.
questions: Why are the image dimensions recorded as 0?
What does 'design ground-truth' specifically mean for this image?
How is this image intended to be consumed by downstream processes?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches: it is a ledger entry for a design-source image asset, though the body adds a ground-truth label not reflected in the structured fields.
intention_signals: To register a specific source image as a trustworthy design reference for the vt-residential project and make it discoverable via the ledger.
contribution: Provides a stable, addressable ledger record that links a blob URI, channel, path, and source label to a design image, enabling retrieval and provenance tracking in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: Ledger entry for the design-source JPEG image vt-residential/p-1, including a blob reference, zero dimensions, and a 'design ground-truth' label.
intention_for_embedding: Catalog a source image as design ground truth for the vt-residential project.
